notes: Durable brewing rules, preferences, and learned insights captured from real use. New entries should be routed into repo state instead of living only in chat memory.

entries.text: During PN9, first wort hops were accidentally treated as mash hops; keep first wort hops and mash hop additions explicitly distinct on brew sheets.
recommended_checks: confirm yeast source, generation, starter method, and dated schedule are explicit
entries.integration_note: Captured into repo state and folded into system_prompt.md.

entries.text: When answering brew-feasibility and inventory questions, avoid wording like 'expensive/limiting'; state the actual coverage or shortage plainly without those labels.
recommended_checks: confirm inventory schema still matches CLI expectations
entries.integration_note: Captured into repo state and folded into system_prompt.md.

entries.text: Inventory correction: rice hulls are not on hand; treat rice_hulls as zero in inventory.
recommended_checks: confirm inventory schema still matches CLI expectations
entries.integration_note: Captured into stock.json and folded into repo inventory state.

entries.text: Time mash-in before turning the pump on, so the pump only starts after the grist is fully wetted and the mash has settled.
entries.integration_note: Captured into repo state and folded into system_prompt.md.

entries.text: When learning from a commercial or pro-brew example, keep the transferable process moves such as water softness, mash simplicity, hop timing, mash-in discipline, gentle recirculation, and fermentation control, but do not copy large-system throughput artifacts blindly.
recommended_checks: review clone and competition-lock recipes for commercial-example process adaptation
entries.integration_note: Captured into repo state and folded into system_prompt.md and README.md.

entries.text: On the G40, stick with a hose return and gentler lower recirculation for now rather than adding a spray ball that might create too much backpressure.
recommended_checks: keep recirculation notes explicit on brew-day sheets
entries.integration_note: Captured into recipe notes and repo process memory.

entries.text: The commercial brewer stages calcium chloride during the mash-side workflow rather than forcing it to be an up-front rule; on the G40, use whichever timing is simplest to execute cleanly.
recommended_checks: keep source-process notes distinct from homebrew execution preferences
entries.integration_note: Captured into recipe notes and repo process memory.

entries.text: Mash recirculation/vorlauf is for clarity and homogeneity, while the whirlpool is a later separate step at the end of the boil even if the same pump path is used.
recommended_checks: label mash recirculation and whirlpool separately on brew-day sheets
entries.integration_note: Captured into recipe notes and repo process memory.

entries.text: Brew-day sheet ingredient rows must not combine multiple physical ingredients on one line. Even batch substitutions such as medium crystal and crystal light must be listed as separate checklist rows so no item is missed during weighing.
recommended_checks: confirm yeast source, generation, starter method, and dated schedule are explicit
entries.integration_note: Captured into system_prompt.md, Copper Crown dated brew sheet, and validate_recipe_brewsheet_sync.py grouped-ingredient checks.

entries.text: When an imported recipe is turned into a brew day sheet, the agentic flow must recreate the full operational brew-day sheet pattern from personal/main examples, not a partial recipe-print sheet. Missing details should be explicit ACTION REQUIRED rows, not omitted sections.
recommended_checks: confirm the intended canonical recipe is still obvious
confirm yeast source, generation, starter method, and dated schedule are explicit
entries.integration_note: Captured into system_prompt.md and validate_print_readability.py operational brew-sheet completeness checks.
